item.title: Iceye Valuation Jumps to €10 Billion in Funding Round Led by General Atlantic
item.description: Iceye Oy quadrupled its valuation to more than €10 billion ($11.5 billion) in a General Atlantic-led funding round that will help the Finnish satellite-intelligence company expand to meet demand for sovereign technology systems.

item.title: Korea Readies On-Site FX Inspections to Curb Won Speculation
item.description: South Korea is set to crack down on speculative foreign-exchange trading, with regulators planning on-site inspections and investigations into suspected market-disruptive activities, the finance ministry said.

item.title: Broadband Firms’ Land Grab Backfired and Banks Are Selling Out
item.description: Banks with exposure to heavily-indebted broadband providers are selling their loans to distressed debt funds at a discount, a sign of their increasing weariness with the cash-strapped sector.

item.title: Vedanta Readies $5.2 Billion Refinancing After Rating Upgrades
item.description: Billionaire Anil Agarwal’s Vedanta Resources Ltd. is set to refinance $5.2 billion of US dollar bonds and loans, according to people familiar with the matter, as it seeks to lower borrowing costs by replacing expensive debt after securing credit-rating upgrades.

item.title: German Industry Output Sees First Gain Since Iran War Began
item.description: German industrial production grew for the first time since war broke out in Iran, fueling hope that Europe’s largest economy is weathering the jump in energy costs.

item.title: Saudi Contractor MGC Postpones $799 Million Riyadh IPO
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Co. has postponed plans for an initial public offering that was set to raise as much as $799 million and rank as the region’s first major listing of the year.

item.title: BlackRock to Offer Space ETF That Will Add New IPOs Within Days
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is launching a new space ETF for European investors that will add newly listed companies within 10 to 30 days.

item.title: Trump Says Peace Talks on Track After Israel-Iran Clashes End
item.description: US President Donald Trump renewed his claims of momentum toward ending the conflict with Iran, after brokering a halt to hostilities between Israel and the Islamic Republic and easing tensions that had threatened to derail broader peace efforts.

item.title: FTSE 100 Falls as Oil and Health Stocks Drag
Indonesia Bolsters Currency Defense With Surprise Rate Hike
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank announced a surprise interest rate hike, seeking to halt a currency freefall that risks stoking inflation.

item.title: Revego and H1 Mull Merger for $807 Million Africa Renewables Fund
item.description: Revego Fund Managers is exploring a merger with H1 Holdings that would create one of South Africa’s largest renewable energy-focused funds.

item.title: CIMB Says ‘Good Time’ to Invest in Battered Indonesia, Seeks M&A
item.description: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd., Malaysia’s second-biggest bank, says it’s seeking merger and acquisition opportunities in neighboring Indonesia despite a slump in investor confidence in Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

item.title: Logistics Firm GLP Aims for $2 Billion of Asset Sales This Year
item.description: GLP Pte. told some bondholders during meetings in recent days that it aims to sell around $2 billion of assets this year, according to people familiar with the matter, as the logistics operator prepares for an initial public offering.

item.title: Poland Eyes New Growth Engines to Compete in Big Leagues, State Assets Minister Says
item.description: Poland wants its local companies to reap the rewards of a projected investment boom as the country seeks new growth drivers to propel its $1 trillion economy, State Assets Minister Wojciech Balczun said.

item.title: Korean Stocks Jump 8% as Chip Shares Rebound After AI Selloff
item.description: Memory chipmaker shares led a rebound in South Korean stocks, demonstrating investors aren’t quite ready to throw in the towel on the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Malaysia Eyes Nearly $200 Billion in Electronics Exports in 2026
item.description: Malaysia’s electrical and electronics exports — which includes semiconductors — could exceed 800 billion ringgit ($197 billion) this year, according to the nation’s chip industry chief.

item.title: Taiwan Five-Year Yields at Highest Since 2008 on Cash, Rate View
item.description: Yields on Taiwan’s five-year government bonds jumped to their highest level since 2008 as tighter banking-system liquidity and growing expectations of higher interest rates weighed on demand for local debt.

item.title: India’s RBI Offers Discounted FX Swaps at 1.5% to Draw Inflows
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India will offer state-run firms a concessional foreign exchange swap facility, allowing them to hedge overseas borrowings at roughly half of the prevailing market costs, according to some analysts.

item.title: China’s Oil Imports Plunge to Eight-Year Low on War Disruptions
item.description: China’s buying of oil from overseas slumped to the lowest in more than eight years last month as the Iran war crimped supply and Beijing held off scrambling for replacement barrels.

item.title: China’s Aluminum Exports Surge to Fill Global Shortfall From War
item.description: Chinese aluminum exports surged in May, helping fill a global shortage caused by war in the Middle East. Crude oil imports continued to slump, dropping to an eight-year low, as the conflict chokes shipments from major producers.

item.title: Japan Still Can’t Escape the Old Normal
item.description: The BOJ has good reasons to hike, but is dragging its feet. A weak yen suits many people.

item.title: What Rolling Back Brexit Would Mean for the UK Economy
item.description: Keir Starmer’s Labour government wants to get closer to Europe, but must choose from a range of options.

item.title: How the UK’s Options for Rebuilding Ties With Europe Stack Up
item.description: Leaving the European Union has cost the UK 2% to 4% of forgone economic output but almost half can be recovered by improving trade with the bloc, according to analysis by Bloomberg Economics for the 10-year anniversary of the Brexit referendum.

item.title: Oil Shock Drives Philippine Underemployment Rate to Near Three-Year High
item.description: The Philippines’ underemployment rate surged to its highest in nearly three years in April even as joblessness eased, with transport workers hit by high oil prices wanting extra hours or jobs.

item.title: Tencent Is Marketing Bonds That May Raise Up to $4.5 Billion
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. began marketing dollar and yuan bonds in an offering that could raise as much as $4.5 billion, people familiar with the matter said, an upward shift from initial thinking following discussions with investors.

item.title: Paytm Plans 10% Staff Boost in AI Pivot With Some Roles Cut
item.description: Indian fintech pioneer Paytm is set to hire about 4,000 people over the next nine months, part of a pivot aimed at expanding its merchant network and artificial intelligence-driven product offerings.

item.title: Indonesia’s Bond Selloff Extends as Five-Year Note Yield Surges
item.description: Indonesia’s debt market selloff deepened as the nation’s five-year bond yield climbed to the highest level in more than six years.

item.title: China Coking Coal Slides After Report on Push to Maintain Output
item.description: Chinese coking coal futures extended declines following a local media report that authorities in Shaanxi asked miners to maintain output, after a deadly accident in neighboring Shanxi last month raised supply and safety concerns.

item.title: Nifty Swings Rise as War Risk Unnerves India Stock Bulls
item.description: Geopolitics, costlier energy and uncertainty over global growth are weighing on sentiment.

item.title: China’s Exports Surge Tops Forecasts as AI Propels Booming Trade
item.description: China’s exports and imports expanded at a faster pace in May, topping forecasts as booming demand for artificial intelligence hardware offsets disruptions from the war in Iran.
